codeReview.summary: CameraView component is well-implemented with proper React patterns, comprehensive error handling, and thorough test coverage. The component successfully integrates camera access via getUserMedia, MediaPipe object detection via useObjectDetection hook, and CoverageOverlay for visualization. All expected behaviors from the feature description are implemented including loading states, error handling for permission denial/unsupported browser/model failures, policy type badge display, and proper cleanup on unmount.

sharedStateObservations.observation: CameraView.jsx uses `../../` relative imports instead of the `@/` path alias convention documented in AGENTS.md. The component imports from '../../context/AppContext.jsx' and '../../hooks/useObjectDetection.js' but should use '@/context/AppContext.jsx' and '@/hooks/useObjectDetection.js' per the documented conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/CameraView/CameraView.jsx lines 2-4: import { useAppContext } from '../../context/AppContext.jsx' and import { useObjectDetection } from '../../hooks/useObjectDetection.js'

summary: CameraView component review: PASS. The component is well-structured with 27 comprehensive tests covering loading states, camera permission handling (denied, retry, manual mode), browser compatibility errors, model loading errors, detection loop functionality, and proper cleanup. Code follows React best practices with proper use of refs, callbacks, and effects. One minor convention observation: the component uses relative imports instead of the @/ alias.
